headline: Gig Workers Exceed 1.3 Million Without Social Protection
context: DOSM reported that gig economy workers in Malaysia surpassed 1.3 million in 2025, with over 78% lacking EPF contributions, SOCSO coverage, or any form of employment insurance.

cards.big: 1.3 million gig workers — Grab, Foodpanda, Lalamove — have zero retirement savings from work
cards.sub: Average gig rider earns RM1,800 monthly but spends RM600 on fuel and vehicle maintenance

cards.big: Gig economy contributes RM50 billion to GDP but platforms pay zero employer social contributions
cards.sub: Companies classify workers as 'partners' to avoid RM3.2 billion in potential EPF/SOCSO obligations

cards.big: 72% of food delivery riders are aged 18-35 — an entire generation building zero pension rights
cards.sub: Injury rates among riders hit 14 per 1,000 workers — 3x higher than manufacturing sector

cards.big: Self-Employment Social Security Act 2017 covers only 8 of 20 identified gig work categories
cards.sub: Human Resources Ministry's i-Saraan voluntary scheme attracted only 145,000 sign-ups by 2025

cards.big: Platforms profit from flexibility — workers pay for it with their retirement security and broken bodies
Like a factory that hires workers through a temp agency to avoid paying benefits — the work is permanent, only the protection is temporary
cards.sub: Platforms structure work as independent contracting to avoid the social protection costs of employment

cards.big: Should gig platforms be required to contribute 13% EPF equivalent for every active worker?
